zotero.abstractNote: We propose a new two-sample test based on the witness function of the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD). While the MMD is a powerful kernel-based test statistic for the two-sample problem, it is often difficult to interpret because it is a single scalar value. By contrast, the witness function provides a localized view of the differences between two distributions. We show that the witness function can be used to construct a powerful two-sample test that is not only interpretable but also competitive with the state-of-the-art. Our test statistic is defined as the supremum of the witness function over a set of functions. We provide a theoretical analysis of the test, including its consistency and asymptotic distribution. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of our test on a variety of synthetic and real-world datasets.
